records.ety: [F., fr. L. spectaculum, fr. spectare to look at, to behold, v. intens. fr. specere. See Spy.]

defs: Something exhibited to view; usually, something presented to view as extraordinary, or as unusual and worthy of special notice; a remarkable or noteworthy sight; a show; a pageant; a gazingstock.
quotes: O, piteous spectacle? O, bloody times!
Poverty a spectacle is, as thinketh me, Through which he may his very friends see.

defs: An optical instrument consisting of two lenses set in a light frame, and worn to assist sight, to obviate some defect in the organs of vision, or to shield the eyes from bright light.
Fig.: An aid to the intellectual sight.
quotes: Shakespeare . . . needed not the spectacles of books to read nature.
